metadata.name: Paladin
metadata.documentation: 1000+ line trading knowledge base with advanced patterns, strategies, and market analysis

bullish_engulfing.name: Bullish Engulfing
bullish_engulfing.description: Strong bullish reversal pattern where a smaller red candle is completely engulfed by a larger green candle forming at the bottom of a downtrend
requirements: Red candle followed by green candle
Green candles body completely engulfs red candle
Occurs after downtrend of at least 3 bars
Volume increase on green candle recommended

signals.entry: Break above green candles high with volume confirmation
signals.stop_loss: Below red candles low or 1.5x ATR below entry

bullish_engulfing.failure_reason: Continued downtrend or retest of pattern lows

bearish_engulfing.name: Bearish Engulfing
bearish_engulfing.description: Strong bearish reversal where a green candle is completely engulfed by a larger red candle forming at top of uptrend
requirements: Green candle followed by red candle
Red candles body completely engulfs green candle
Occurs after uptrend of at least 3 bars
Volume increase on red candle preferred

signals.entry: Break below red candles low with volume
signals.stop_loss: Above green candles high or 1.5x ATR above entry

bearish_engulfing.occurrence_frequency: Appears 2-3 times per month in major pairs

morning_star.name: Morning Star
morning_star.description: Three candle bullish reversal consisting of long red candle, small body gap down candle, then strong green candle closing above midpoint of first candles body
visual_characteristics: Gap down between first and second candle
Second candle has small body doji like appearance
Third candle is strong bullish reversal
requirements: Small body candle below trend gapping down
Strong green candle closing above midpoint of first red candle
Volume should increase on third candle
Gap between first and second candles

signals.entry: On close of third green candle above resistance level
signals.stop_loss: Below doji candle low or 2x ATR
signals.take_profit: Previous resistance or 3x ATR from entry

confirmation_methods: Confirmation next candle should stay above middle
Support level at second candles body

evening_star.name: Evening Star
evening_star.description: Three candle bearish reversal consisting of long green candle small body candle above then strong red candle closing below midpoint of first green candles body
requirements: Small body candle above trend gapping up
Strong red candle closing below midpoint of first green candle

signals.entry: Join close of third red candle below support
signals.take_profit: Previous support or 3x ATR from entry

hammer.name: Hammer
hammer.description: Single candle with small real body at top of range and a long lower shadow typically 2-3x the body size forming at bottom of downtrend

characteristics.color: Typically bullish but color less important

requirements: After downtrend or in support area

signals.entry: Confirmation with next green candle closing above hammer high
signals.stop_loss: Below hammer low or below shadow extreme

hanging_man.name: Hanging Man
hanging_man.description: Single candle at top of uptrend with small body at upper end and long lower shadow forming potential reversal signal
requirements: Small real body at top of range

signals.entry: Confirmation with bearish candle breaking below hanging mans body

inverted_hammer.name: Inverted Hammer
inverted_hammer.description: Single candle with small body and long upper shadow 2-3x body size with minimal lower shadow at bottom of downtrend
requirements: After downtrend or in support area

doji.name: Doji
doji.description: Open and close prices nearly equal with long upper and lower shadows creating cross like appearance indicating indecision
subtypes: Spinning top - small body with long shadows

signals.entry: Directional candle following doji in trending direction

three_line_strike.name: Three Line Strike
three_line_strike.description: Three consecutive candles trending same direction followed by reversal candle that closes beyond first candles extreme
three_line_strike.occurrence: 1-2 times per week in active pairs
requirements: Three consecutive candles trending same direction
Fourth candle reverses and closes beyond first candles opening
Fourth candle larger than previous three

signals.entry: Reversal candle close or break beyond previous extreme

rsi.name: Relative Strength Index
rsi.formula: RSI = 100 - (100 / (1 + RS)) where RS = Avg Gain / Avg Loss

signals.divergence: Price makes new high but RSI doesnt indicates weakness
signals.bullish_divergence: Price makes new low but RSI doesnt indicates strength

interpretation: Use with other indicators for confirmation
Period can adjust for faster or slower readings
false_signals: Can stay overbought in strong uptrends
Can stay oversold in strong downtrends

macd.name: Moving Average Convergence Divergence
macd.calculation: MACD = 12 EMA - 26 EMA, Signal = 9 EMA of MACD, Histogram = MACD - Signal

signals.bullish_cross: MACD crosses above signal line strong buy
signals.bearish_cross: MACD crosses below signal line strong sell
signals.histogram_positive: MACD above signal green histogram bullish
signals.histogram_negative: MACD below signal red histogram bearish
signals.zero_line_cross: Center line cross indicates trend change

bollinger_bands.name: Bollinger Bands
bollinger_bands.calculation: Middle = 20 SMA, Upper = Middle + (2 * StdDev), Lower = Middle - (2 * StdDev)

signals.expansion: Bands widen volatility increasing breakout coming
signals.walk_upper: Strong bullish trend price walking upper band
signals.walk_lower: Strong bearish trend price walking lower band

bollinger_bands.adaptability: Auto adjusts to volatility in real time

trend_following.name: Trend Following Strategy
entry_conditions: Price above 50 EMA above 200 EMA established uptrend
RSI between 40-70 not overbought
MACD above zero and bullish histogram
ADX > 25 confirming strong trend
Volume above average confirming moves
trend_following.entry_trigger: Price action pullback to 20 EMA then bullish reversal candle
trend_following.stop_loss: 1.5x ATR or below swing low below entry
rules: Only trade direction of established trend
Buy pullbacks to EMA not highs
Scale out of positions into strength
Use ATR for dynamic stops and targets

mean_reversion.name: Mean Reversion Strategy
entry_conditions: RSI > 70 or RSI < 30 extreme levels
Price at Bollinger Band extremes upper or lower
Stochastic > 80 or < 20 overbought oversold
Price pullback from recent high or bounce from low
Volume decreasing indicating exhaustion
mean_reversion.entry_trigger: Candle close back inside bands or reversal pattern forms
mean_reversion.take_profit: Return to 20 EMA or previous swing level
rules: Take profits at mean not extreme
Use tight stops in reversal zones
Confirm with multiple indicators
Avoid fading in strong trends

breakout.name: Breakout Strategy
breakout.definition: Trade the break of resistance or support levels with volume confirmation
entry_conditions: Price above resistance or below support confirmed
Volume increase > 2x average expanding
ADX > 25 strong trend forming
Bollinger Band expansion showing volatility increase
Breakout on high volume not low volume
breakout.entry_trigger: Close beyond previous highs for long or lows for short
breakout.stop_loss: Below breakout level or 2x ATR buffer
rules: Volume must confirm breakout
No breakout in support without volume
Watch for false breakout and reversal
Take partial profits quick at first target

support_resistance.name: Support Resistance Trading
entry_conditions: Price at historical support or resistance level identified
Pullback within 20% of swing point from extreme
Reversal pattern forming hammer doji engulfing
Volume decreasing at level indicating indecision
RSI or other indicator showing bounce setup
support_resistance.entry_trigger: Candlestick reversal pattern at support resistance level
support_resistance.position_sizing: 2% per trade good risk reward potential
support_resistance.stop_loss: Below support or 1.5x ATR below entry for longs
support_resistance.take_profit: Next resistance level or 2x ATR from entry
rules: Identify key levels weekly monthly
Watch first bounce at new level
Move to breakeven after 1x ATR profit
Use multiple time frame analysis for confirmation

position_sizing.rule: Risk only 1-2% of account per trade maximum
position_sizing.example: 10000 account 1% risk entry 100 stop 95 equals 20 shares risked
position_sizing.adjustment: Reduce size in losing streaks increase in hot streaks
position_sizing.kelly_criterion: (Win% * Avg Win - Loss% * Avg Loss) / Avg Loss
position_sizing.half_kelly: Use half kelly for safety in trading

risk_reward.calculation: Risk Reward = Distance to TP / Distance to SL
risk_reward.principle: Do not trade risk reward less than 1.5 ever

methods: Support based below recent support or swing low
Indicator based below opposite band or EMA
Time based exit after X bars if no movement
Hard stops never move wrong direction
stop_loss.golden_rule: Always use stop loss never trade without one
stop_loss.mental_stops: NOT acceptable must be hard stops in system

levels: Trailing: After 1x ATR profit move stop to breakeven then trail
take_profit.philosophy: Greed kills trading accounts take profits locked in

fear.cause: Large losses losing streak market dropping
fear.impact: Exit winners too early scalp profits cut runs short
fear.solution: Strict trading plan follow system rules always
fear.prevention: Risk management rules and position sizing

greed.cause: Winning trades feeling invincible FOMO big moves
greed.impact: Over size positions revenge trade no stop loss
greed.solution: Stick rules don't increase size after wins
greed.trading_rule: Position size same regardless of confidence

overconfidence.cause: Winning streak perfect trades in row
overconfidence.impact: Ignore risk rules increase size don't plan
overconfidence.solution: Track win rate sharpe ratio not just pnl
overconfidence.reality_check: Even best traders have 50-60% win rate

frustration.impact: Revenge trading bad setups overtrading revenge
frustration.solution: Step away take break return fresh

rules: Do not trade emotional only when setup is clear
Do not average down losing positions ever
Do not revenge trade after loss take break
Do not FOMO chase breakouts missing setup
Dont quit winners early from fear it hurts return
Dont ignore stop losses ever without exception
Track EVERY trade in journal analyze weekly
Review trades and improve learn from mistakes
Take breaks mental state is critical

order_flow_analysis.bid_ask_analysis: Study buying and selling pressure at price levels
order_flow_analysis.volume_profile: Volume clusters at specific price levels indicate support/resistance
order_flow_analysis.market_microstructure: Large orders split into many small orders to hide impact
order_flow_analysis.time_and_sales: Every tick represents a transaction at specific price and volume
order_flow_analysis.level_2_data: See all bids and asks in the order book

elliot_wave_theory.wave_2: Pullback but not below wave 1 start
elliot_wave_theory.wave_4: Pullback but overlaps wave 1 low rule
elliot_wave_theory.wave_5: Final push up smaller than wave 3

market_profile.value_area: Where 70% of trading occurred in session

cognitive_biases: Confirmation bias - seeking info that confirms beliefs
Recency bias - recent events weighted too heavily
Hindsight bias - thinking past was obvious
Anchoring bias - fixating on numbers like previous highs
Sunk cost fallacy - holding losers to recover costs

risk_aversion.problem: Traders too afraid of losses take profits too early
risk_aversion.solution: Accept 30-40% losing trades as cost of business
risk_aversion.mindset: Focus on risk reward ratios not hit rates

discipline_building: Trade the same size every trade
Follow setup rules exactly no exceptions
Keep trading journal of every trade
Stick to high probability setups only

characteristics: Higher highs and higher lows pattern
MACD above zero and bullish histogram
Lower highs and lower lows pattern
MACD below zero and bearish histogram
ADX < 20 weak trend no directional bias
Higher lows and lower highs trapped

performance_metrics.sortino_ratio: Return per unit of downside volatility

three_stars_in_the_south.name: Three Stars in the South
three_stars_in_the_south.description: Three candles forming in a bearish trend — each subsequent candle has a smaller body and shadow than the previous, showing declining bearish momentum
three_stars_in_the_south.candle_1: Long black candle with long lower shadow
three_stars_in_the_south.candle_2: Smaller black candle, holds above candle 1 low
three_stars_in_the_south.candle_3: Small black marubozu completely within candle 2 range
three_stars_in_the_south.psychology: Bears losing control progressively — each bar shows less and less selling force

ladder_bottom.name: Ladder Bottom
ladder_bottom.description: Five-candle bullish reversal: three long black candles with rising opens (staircase down), then bearish candle with long upper shadow, then bullish confirmation
ladder_bottom.note: The long upper shadow on candle 4 is the key — bears are failing to sustain the downward momentum

break_away_pattern.name: Breakaway Pattern
break_away_pattern.description: Five candles. Bullish version: long black, black with gap, two smaller blacks, then strong white candle closing into the gap
break_away_pattern.note: The return into the gap and reclaim signals buyers have taken control

two_crows.name: Two Crows
two_crows.description: Three-candle bearish reversal in uptrend: long white, gap-up black candle, larger black candle engulfing the second and closing within first
two_crows.psychology: Gap up enthusiasm immediately overwhelmed by sellers on the third candle

three_stars_south.name: Tri-Star Doji
three_stars_south.description: Three doji candles in succession — maximum sustained indecision
three_stars_south.note: Three consecutive doji is extremely rare. When it occurs, a major move is imminent. Trade the breakout direction.

on_neck.name: On-Neck Pattern
on_neck.description: Bearish continuation: long black candle, then small white candle closing at or near the CLOSE (not low) of the first
on_neck.note: Bulls could only recover to the prior close — still bearish bias

in_neck.name: In-Neck Pattern
in_neck.description: Similar to on-neck but white candle closes slightly above the black candle's close
in_neck.note: Slightly more bullish than on-neck but still bearish continuation context

thrusting.name: Thrusting Pattern
thrusting.description: Like on-neck and in-neck but white candle pushes into lower half of prior black body
thrusting.note: Fails to pierce above midpoint — still bearish overall

rising_window.name: Rising Window (Bullish Gap)
rising_window.description: Bullish gap in uptrend — a window opens between two candles
rising_window.trading: Buy retest of the window (gap level acts as support)

falling_window.name: Falling Window (Bearish Gap)
falling_window.trading: Short retest of window from below

upside_gap_two_crows.name: Upside Gap Two Crows
upside_gap_two_crows.description: Long white candle, then two black candles that gap up but then fail to sustain — final black candle closes within the first white candle's body

tasuki_gap.name: Tasuki Gap
tasuki_gap.description: Two candles in trend direction with a gap, then a third candle of opposite color that fails to close the gap
tasuki_gap.bullish_tasuki: Two up candles with gap, then black candle fails to close gap = bullish continuation
tasuki_gap.bearish_tasuki: Two down candles with gap, then white candle fails to close gap = bearish continuation

naked_price_action.philosophy: Remove all indicators. Price itself tells you everything. Indicators are derivatives of price — they are always lagging.

the_bar.open: Where the market DECIDED to start — gap from previous close reveals institutional overnight intent
the_bar.high: The maximum optimism or maximum short covering during the period
the_bar.low: The maximum pessimism or maximum long liquidation during the period
the_bar.close: The final verdict — where informed money decided to end the period. THE MOST IMPORTANT PRICE.

pin_bars.description: Candles with a long 'pin' (wick) on one end, rejecting a price level
pin_bars.bullish_pin: Long lower wick after downtrend or at support — price was pushed down but completely rejected
pin_bars.bearish_pin: Long upper wick after uptrend or at resistance — price was pushed up but rejected
criteria: Pin/wick must be at least 2x the body size
Wick points TOWARD the rejected level
Must occur in context (at S/R, after trend)
Ideally, wick protrudes beyond neighboring bars (pierces key level)

fakey_setup.name: Fakey Setup (False Breakout)
fakey_setup.description: Inside bar forms, price breaks one side briefly, fails, then reverses strongly through the OTHER side
fakey_setup.psychology: Traders were trapped by the false breakout — their stops fuel the real move
fakey_setup.entry: Trade the reversal through the opposite side of the inside bar
fakey_setup.creator: Nial Fuller

outside_bar.description: A bar whose high exceeds the previous high AND low exceeds the previous low — the 'outside' or engulfing bar at chart pattern level
outside_bar.bullish_outside: If closes near high = very bullish engulfing of prior period
outside_bar.bearish_outside: If closes near low = very bearish
outside_bar.note: Same as engulfing at candlestick level but at the bar/price action level

inside_bar.description: Bar completely within the previous bar's range — consolidation
inside_bar.breakout_direction: Trade the break in direction of trend for continuation
inside_bar.reversal_use: After trend, an inside bar cluster at resistance = potential reversal
inside_bar.multiple_inside_bars: More inside bars in sequence = more compressed energy = more explosive breakout

two_bar_reversal.description: Two bars that visually 'flip' — one directional candle immediately followed by an equal and opposite candle
two_bar_reversal.psychology: Total momentum reversal in one period

order_block_theory.name: Order Block Theory (ICT Concepts)
order_block_theory.creator: Inner Circle Trader (Michael Huddleston)
order_block_theory.description: Advanced institutional price action theory focusing on where institutional orders were placed and will be revisited

bullish_order_block.definition: The last bearish (down) candle before a significant bullish move — where institutional buyers were accumulating
bullish_order_block.identification: Find a strong bullish move. Go back to the last red candle before the move began. That is the order block.
bullish_order_block.entry: When price returns to the order block area, buy
bullish_order_block.logic: Institutions couldn't fill all their orders on the initial move — they wait for price to return to fill the remainder

bearish_order_block.definition: The last bullish (up) candle before a significant bearish move
bearish_order_block.entry: When price returns to the area, short

fair_value_gap.description: Three-candle formation where candle 1's high and candle 3's low don't overlap — leaving an 'imbalance' or gap in price
fair_value_gap.bullish_fvg: Candle 1 low is higher than candle 3 high — bullish imbalance zone. Price tends to return to fill it.
fair_value_gap.bearish_fvg: Candle 1 high is lower than candle 3 low — bearish imbalance zone
fair_value_gap.trading: Treat FVG as a support/resistance zone — price returns to fill the gap before continuing the trend

breaker_block.description: When an order block is consumed (price breaks through and closes beyond it), it becomes a 'breaker block' — resistance flipped to support or vice versa
breaker_block.trading: Trade the retest of a broken order block from the other side

order_block_theory.note: ICT concepts are advanced and contentious. The underlying logic of institutional accumulation/distribution at specific levels is sound, but requires extensive practice to apply correctly.

backtesting_principles.what_is_backtesting: Testing a trading strategy against historical data to determine if it would have produced a statistical edge
requirements_for_valid_backtest: Sufficient historical data (minimum 200+ trades for statistical significance)
Out-of-sample testing (test on data NOT used to develop the system)
No look-ahead bias (only using data available at the time of each trade)
Multiple market regimes (test across bull, bear, and ranging markets)

key_metrics.win_rate: Percentage of winning trades. Meaningless without knowing R:R. 40% win rate at 3:1 RR is fine.

profit_factor.above_3: Outstanding — typically too good, check for errors

expectancy.formula: (Win Rate × Avg Win) - (Loss Rate × Avg Loss)
expectancy.target: Positive expectancy required. Higher = better edge per trade.

sharpe_ratio.note: Higher = more return per unit of risk. The gold standard of risk-adjusted performance.

max_drawdown.acceptable: Max drawdown should not exceed 3x the typical losing period return
max_drawdown.rule: If you can't psychologically tolerate the max drawdown, you will abandon the system at the worst time

key_metrics.consecutive_losses: Know the maximum consecutive losing streak historically. You WILL experience this eventually. Be prepared.

walkforward_testing.description: Divide data into in-sample (optimization) and out-of-sample (testing) periods and test forward
walkforward_testing.method: Optimize on first 70% of data. Test blindly on last 30%.
walkforward_testing.more_rigorous: Rolling walkforward: optimize quarterly, test on next quarter, roll forward

monte_carlo_simulation.description: Randomly shuffle trade order thousands of times to see distribution of outcomes
monte_carlo_simulation.purpose: Understand worst-case scenarios beyond historical max drawdown
monte_carlo_simulation.use: Size positions based on Monte Carlo worst-case, not historical worst-case

building_an_edge.definition_of_edge: A statistical advantage that, applied consistently over many trades, produces positive expectancy
sources_of_edge: Pattern recognition — identifiable patterns with above-random win rates
Risk management — superior risk/reward ratios give edge even with low win rates
Behavioral edge — discipline when others are emotional
Informational edge — better market analysis through multi-timeframe, multi-market analysis
Timing edge — entering and exiting at better prices through limit orders and precision

edge_erosion.description: Edges tend to erode over time as they become widely known or market structure changes
combating_erosion: Continuously research and update your approach
Monitor system performance — if win rate or expectancy drops significantly, investigate

building_an_edge.sample_size: Most strategy edges require 50+ trades minimum to determine if the edge is real. Do not judge after 5 trades.

daily_metrics: Number of A+ setups seen vs taken
monthly_review: Psychological review — emotional states vs outcomes

application.method: Count from significant turning points. At Fibonacci number of bars, be alert for potential turns.
application.combining: When Fibonacci TIME aligns with Fibonacci PRICE, the probability of a turn is dramatically enhanced
examples: 13 days after a major high — watch for a secondary test or continuation
34 weeks from a major low — potential next major turning point
89 months from a financial crisis — potential next major cycle low

price_confluence_mapping.description: Draw Fibonacci from every significant swing in each direction. Mark all levels. Where multiple levels converge = high probability zone.
process: Draw Fib retracements from all major swings (up and down) on the chart
Draw Fib extensions from all three-point setups
Mark every level on the chart
Identify 'clusters' where 3+ levels coincide within a narrow band
These clusters are your highest probability support/resistance zones

fibonacci_fans.description: Diagonal Fibonacci-based levels drawn from significant pivot points
fibonacci_fans.drawing: From a significant low, draw lines at Fibonacci angles (38.2%, 50%, 61.8%) to prior highs
fibonacci_fans.use: Dynamic support levels that evolve as time passes — useful for trending markets

fibonacci_arcs.description: Curved Fibonacci levels emanating from a significant pivot — combines price AND time
fibonacci_arcs.use: Shows where price might encounter support/resistance based on both dimensions

fibonacci_spirals.description: Logarithmic spirals based on Fibonacci ratios — used by advanced technicians for major cycle analysis
fibonacci_spirals.note: Complex but can identify large-scale price structure in major indices and commodities

gann_analysis.name: W.D. Gann Analysis
gann_analysis.overview: W.D. Gann (1878-1955) developed a highly complex and still controversial trading methodology combining geometry, time cycles, and astrology

gann_angles.definition: Lines drawn from significant highs or lows at specific angles representing the relationship between price and time

1_to_1.name: The 1x1 Gann Angle (45 degrees)
1_to_1.meaning: Price moves 1 unit for every 1 unit of time — the most important Gann angle
1_to_1.significance: The primary trend line. As long as price is above the 1x1 angle from a major low, the trend is bullish.

other_angles: 2x1: Price moves 2 points per time unit — very steep angle
1x2: Price moves 1 point per 2 time units — moderate angle
4x1: Very steep, usually associated with parabolic moves
gann_angles.rule: When price breaks through one Gann angle, it tends to move to the next angle

gann_square_of_9.description: A spiral of numbers where 360-degree movements in the square correspond to support/resistance levels
gann_square_of_9.use: Price targets and reversal points based on geometric price relationships
gann_square_of_9.note: Highly complex and subjective — mastery requires years of dedicated study

important_cycles: 144 calendar days (Gann's most important short cycle)
gann_time_cycles.anniversary_dates: Major turns often occur on anniversary dates of previous major turns

market_order.description: Execute immediately at current market price
market_order.use_when: Speed is more important than price — high momentum breakouts
market_order.risk: Slippage in illiquid markets or during news events. Always know bid/ask before using.
market_order.never_use: In illiquid markets, on thinly traded stocks, during news releases

limit_order.description: Execute only at specified price or better
limit_order.use_when: Entering at specific levels (support, Fibonacci, pattern completion)
limit_order.risk: Might not get filled — price might not return to your limit

stop_order.description: Becomes a market order when price reaches the stop level
stop_order.risk: Slippage — in fast markets, execution can be far beyond the stop price

stop_limit_order.description: Becomes a LIMIT order (not market) when triggered
stop_limit_order.risk: Might not get filled if price blows through quickly
stop_limit_order.use: Stop losses in liquid markets where slippage is a concern

trailing_stop_order.description: Stop that moves automatically in your favor by a set amount

oco_order.name: One Cancels Other (OCO)
oco_order.description: Two orders — when one fills, the other is automatically cancelled
oco_order.use: Set both profit target and stop loss simultaneously — when one fills, the other cancels
oco_order.professional: ALL professionals use OCO — manually managing both exits is error-prone

bracket_order.description: Entry order with automatic profit target and stop loss attached
bracket_order.advantage: Removes need to manually place stops after entry — critical for risk management
bracket_order.use: For all planned trades — enter the trade fully managed from the start

aggressive_entry.description: Enter on the signal candle close or immediately at a level
aggressive_entry.advantage: Better entry price — closer to stop = better R:R
aggressive_entry.when_to_use: Strong confluence setups, clear signal candles, liquid markets

conservative_entry.description: Wait for confirmation (next candle, volume, structure break)
conservative_entry.risk: Worse entry price, sometimes miss the trade
conservative_entry.when_to_use: Weaker setups, illiquid markets, when conviction is moderate

limit_order_at_level.description: Place limit order at the exact level BEFORE price arrives
limit_order_at_level.advantage: Best possible entry price, no chasing, automatic execution
limit_order_at_level.risk: Must identify level correctly in advance — requires planning
limit_order_at_level.professional_use: Most institutional entries are limit orders placed in advance

slippage_management.definition: Difference between expected entry price and actual execution price
mitigation: Trade liquid instruments with tight bid/ask spreads
Avoid market orders on thin or high-volatility instruments
Factor expected slippage into your risk/reward calculations

enter_before_the_break.description: Place limit orders INSIDE the pattern — anticipate the breakout rather than chasing it
enter_before_the_break.advantage: Much better entry price, stop is tighter
enter_before_the_break.risk: Pattern might not complete — get stopped out before the real move
enter_before_the_break.use_case: When pattern and context are extremely clear, and price is testing the breakout level

enter_on_the_break.description: Buy stop just above resistance or sell stop just below support
enter_on_the_break.advantage: Only enter if breakout is confirmed
enter_on_the_break.risk: Buy at the break = buy near high of move so far. More expensive.
enter_on_the_break.use_case: Most common approach. Trade the break.

enter_on_the_retest.description: Wait for breakout, wait for retest of broken level, enter on the retest
enter_on_the_retest.risk: Retest doesn't always come — miss the trade
enter_on_the_retest.statistics: Retests occur 60% of the time on genuine breakouts
enter_on_the_retest.use_case: When breakout confirmation is required and you have patience

market_manipulation_awareness.overview: Understanding how markets can be manipulated — legal and illegal — protects traders from being on the wrong side of institutional games

stop_hunting.description: Price pushed to predictable stop locations to trigger orders, then reversed
how_to_identify: Price pushes just beyond an obvious level (round number, prior high/low)
Price immediately reverses and closes back within the range
The move looks 'too clean' — perfectly hits the level and bounces
protection: Place stops BEYOND obvious levels — not AT them
Use ATR-based stops that are less predictable
Consider buffer zones (e.g., stop at -1.25x ATR instead of -1x ATR)

pump_and_dump.description: Coordinated buying to inflate price artificially, then selling into the inflated price to unsuspecting buyers
signs: Sudden parabolic move with no news catalyst
Extremely high volume out of nowhere
Social media hype coordinated with price move
pump_and_dump.protection: Never chase parabolic moves. If you can't identify a legitimate catalyst, stay out.

short_squeeze.description: Stock with high short interest rises sharply, forcing shorts to cover, which creates more buying, creating a feedback loop

trading.long_squeeze: Identify highly shorted stocks (short interest > 20% of float) making new highs — potential squeeze fuel
trading.warning_for_shorts: NEVER short a stock with extremely high short interest unless you have extreme conviction — squeeze risk is asymmetric

short_squeeze.gamma_squeeze: Options-driven version — large call option buying forces market makers to hedge by buying stock, accelerating the move

spoofing.description: Placing large orders with no intention of filling them — to create false impression of supply/demand, then pulling the order
spoofing.legality: Illegal under US and EU regulations — but still occurs
spoofing.how_to_spot: Large orders appearing in Level 2 that repeatedly disappear before being hit
spoofing.protection: Do not trade based on Level 2 orders alone — they can be spoofed

wash_trading.description: Simultaneously buying and selling the same instrument to create artificial volume
wash_trading.prevalence: Very common in crypto markets — some exchanges report inflated volumes
wash_trading.protection: Cross-reference volume figures across multiple exchanges for crypto

mean_reversion.description: VIX is mean-reverting — extreme spikes always revert
mean_reversion.strategy: When VIX spikes above 40-50, buy equity index futures or ETFs (SPY, QQQ)
mean_reversion.mechanism: Extreme fear creates oversold conditions that reverse
mean_reversion.historical_success: Buying equity indices when VIX > 40 has been profitable in almost every historical instance
mean_reversion.timing: Don't try to catch the exact bottom — VIX can spike further before reverting

vix_futures_term_structure.contango: Front month VIX < second month — normal. VIX ETFs (VXX) decay over time in contango.
vix_futures_term_structure.backwardation: Front month VIX > second month — crisis conditions. VIX ETFs gain value.

trading.contango_trade: Short VXX or similar VIX-long ETPs in prolonged contango periods for roll yield decay
trading.risk: Catastrophic loss if VIX spikes while short — always hedge or use small size

vix_divergence.description: Stock market making new highs while VIX is also rising — unusual and bearish
vix_divergence.signal: Fear is building beneath the surface even as price rises — potential blow-off top
vix_divergence.action: Reduce long exposure, consider protective puts

implied_vs_realized_volatility.iv: Implied Volatility — what the options market EXPECTS future volatility to be (derived from option prices)
implied_vs_realized_volatility.rv: Realized (Historical) Volatility — what volatility actually WAS over a past period

vol_premium.description: Options implied volatility is almost always ABOVE realized volatility — this 'vol premium' is the theoretical edge for options sellers
vol_premium.data: Historically, IV exceeds RV 80%+ of the time — this is why options selling strategies have positive expectancy
vol_premium.risk: When tail events occur, RV can massively exceed IV — devastating for naked options sellers

trading_the_premium.entry_criteria: IV Rank > 50 — selling options is most attractive when IV is historically elevated

gamma_scalping.description: Advanced options strategy: buy ATM straddles, then delta-hedge by trading the underlying — collecting gamma as the underlying moves
gamma_scalping.profit_from: Actual volatility (RV) exceeding the implied volatility paid
gamma_scalping.requires: Active, frequent hedging. Sophisticated understanding of options greeks.
gamma_scalping.note: Professional market-making technique — not for beginners

random_walk_hypothesis.name: Random Walk Hypothesis (Burton Malkiel, 1965)
random_walk_hypothesis.claim: Price movements are random and cannot be predicted
random_walk_hypothesis.trader_response: Partially true for short-term noise, but FALSE for trend persistence and pattern behavior at key levels
random_walk_hypothesis.evidence_against: 60+ years of trend following profits, momentum anomaly, mean reversion strategies — all inconsistent with pure random walk

efficient_market_hypothesis.name: Efficient Market Hypothesis (Eugene Fama, 1965)
efficient_market_hypothesis.weak_form: All past price information is already in prices — technical analysis cannot work
efficient_market_hypothesis.strong_form: All public and private information is in prices — no edge possible
efficient_market_hypothesis.practical_reality: Markets are MOSTLY efficient, not PERFECTLY efficient. The gaps between mostly and perfectly — the inefficiencies — are where trader profits come from.

black_scholes_options_pricing.significance: Created options pricing framework, enabling modern options markets
black_scholes_options_pricing.trader_impact: Options became investable instruments — hedging and speculation with defined risk became possible
black_scholes_options_pricing.practical: Understand options are priced using implied volatility — when IV is high, options are expensive. When low, they're cheap.

commodity_markets_evolution.description: 1970s saw massive commodity bull markets driven by inflation — oil embargo, food inflation, gold rally after Bretton Woods collapse
commodity_markets_evolution.lesson: Commodity supercycles last many years and are driven by supply/demand imbalances that take years to resolve
commodity_markets_evolution.trader_lesson: Major macro trends — inflation, deflation, commodity super-cycles — are the biggest trends in the market and should be traded with the largest size

portfolio_insurance.description: Strategy using futures to hedge equity portfolios — mechanically selling as markets fell
portfolio_insurance.1987_crash_role: Portfolio insurance created a feedback loop — falling prices triggered selling, which drove prices further, triggering more selling
portfolio_insurance.lesson: Mechanical strategies adopted by many participants simultaneously can create systemic risk

junk_bond_era.description: High-yield bond market development enabled leveraged buyouts
junk_bond_era.lesson: Credit cycles are as important as equity cycles — watch credit spreads
junk_bond_era.high_yield_spread: When high-yield spreads widen rapidly, equity bear markets often follow

turtle_traders_experiment.description: Richard Dennis and William Eckhardt bet that great traders could be taught in 2 weeks
turtle_traders_experiment.result: The Turtles went on to collectively make hundreds of millions using a trend-following system
turtle_traders_experiment.lesson: Trading ability CAN be taught — it is primarily discipline and rule-following, not innate talent
turtle_rules_summary: Trade breakouts from 20-day and 55-day highs and lows
Add to winners at every new ATR in your favor
Exit on 10-day breakout counter to position
Never trade against a confirmed trend

emerging_markets_crises.mexico_1994: Peso crisis — currency peg collapse under current account pressure
emerging_markets_crises.asia_1997: Multiple Asian currency pegs collapsed simultaneously — contagion effect
emerging_markets_crises.russia_1998: Sovereign default — LTCM nearly brought down the global financial system
lessons: Currency pegs that are unsustainable WILL break — the only question is when
Contagion is real — one market's crisis can spread to seemingly unrelated markets
Leverage + concentrated risk = catastrophic failure, no matter how sophisticated the participants

characteristics: Companies with no profits or revenue trading at billions
IPOs doubling on first day of trading as a regular occurrence
New era thinking — 'this time is different'
dotcom_bubble_formation.trader_lesson: Extreme valuation + unanimous sentiment + price exhaustion patterns = major top. The higher the euphoria, the harder the fall.

housing_and_credit_bubble.mechanism: Loose credit standards, structured credit products, leverage, and feedback loop in housing prices
signals_that_were_there: Housing prices rising faster than income growth
Credit default swap spreads beginning to rise
housing_and_credit_bubble.lesson: Credit bubbles are the most dangerous bubbles — the unwinding is not just equity prices, it's the entire credit system

2008_crash_lessons: Correlation goes to 1 in a crisis — all risky assets fall together
Liquidity is everything — illiquid assets cannot be sold at any price in a crisis
Government and central bank response is the key variable in crisis recovery
The best opportunities come at the moment of maximum fear — March 2009 was the buying opportunity of a generation

quantitative_easing_era.description: Federal Reserve and global central banks expanded balance sheets to historic levels
quantitative_easing_era.market_impact: Artificially low rates pushed investors into risk assets — 'TINA' (There Is No Alternative to stocks)
quantitative_easing_era.trader_adaptation: The traditional relationship between valuation and price broke down — higher valuation justified by lower discount rate
quantitative_easing_era.lesson: Understand the monetary environment you are trading in — it changes which strategies work

algorithmic_trading_dominance.statistics: Estimated 70-80% of US equity volume is now algorithmic
implications_for_traders: Price action at obvious levels is often algorithmic — stops are hunted mechanically
News-driven moves happen in milliseconds — traders cannot react to news
HFT creates specific microstructure patterns that can be exploited
Mean reversion strategies may work better after HFT-driven overshoots

pandemic_trading.march_2020: Fastest bear market in history — 34% decline in 33 days
pandemic_trading.recovery: Fastest recovery from bear market in history — driven by unprecedented fiscal and monetary stimulus
lessons: Liquidity provision by central banks can override fundamentals in the short term
When every participant expects catastrophe, the surprise is often the recovery
Trading requires adapting to completely new environments — historical relationships can temporarily break

meme_stock_era.description: Retail traders coordinating through social media to squeeze heavily shorted stocks
meme_stock_era.gamestop_2021: GameStop rose from $20 to $483 in weeks due to coordinated retail buying of calls
lessons: Social sentiment can create short-term moves that are completely disconnected from fundamentals
Short squeezes in heavily shorted small caps remain dangerous for shorts
Options market structure (gamma squeezes) can amplify moves in ways never seen before

crypto_and_defi_evolution.description: Cryptocurrency and decentralized finance emerged as legitimate asset classes
characteristics_for_traders: Extreme volatility — standard equity risk management must be dramatically adjusted
Correlation with risk appetite — bitcoin tracks Nasdaq during risk-off periods
New metrics (on-chain analytics, tokenomics) provide additional analysis tools

essential_tools: Drawing tools: trendlines, Fibonacci, horizontal levels
Alert system for price levels and indicator triggers
One-click trading with preset position sizes

charting_platforms.tradingview: Most popular retail charting platform. Excellent community indicators. Web-based.
charting_platforms.thinkorswim: TD Ameritrade/Schwab platform. Excellent for options analysis.
charting_platforms.sierra_chart: Professional-grade. Best for order flow and depth of market analysis.
charting_platforms.ninjatrader: Popular for futures. Good automation capability.

execution_platforms.interactive_brokers: Best for professional traders — lowest commissions, best margin rates, most instruments
execution_platforms.tradestation: Good for systematic traders. Easyganguage programming.
execution_platforms.note: Execution platform choice can significantly impact profitability through commission structure and execution quality

semi_automated.description: Use automation for routine tasks — alerts, order placement, position sizing — while human judges the setup
semi_automated.tools: Most platforms support alert-triggered bracket orders
semi_automated.advantage: Removes manual execution errors, enforces risk management rules mechanically

fully_automated.description: Algorithm generates signals AND executes trades without human intervention
requirements: Robust backtesting across multiple market regimes
Execution infrastructure (VPS, API connection to broker)
fully_automated.risk: Systems can break in new market regimes. Regular monitoring essential.

python_for_traders.basic_workflow: Download data → Calculate indicators → Generate signals → Backtest → Optimize → Paper trade → Live trade

alternative_data.description: Non-traditional data sources for additional edge
examples: Satellite imagery of retail parking lots (predict sales before earnings)
alternative_data.note: Alternative data is increasingly used by sophisticated hedge funds — awareness helps understand market moves

sector_rotation_model_detailed.description: A detailed framework for rotating between sectors as the economic cycle progresses

technology.characteristics: High growth, reinvests earnings, sensitive to interest rates
technology.worst_phase: Rate hike cycles (high discount rate hurts growth stocks)
technology.indicators: Semiconductor orders, enterprise software spending, cloud growth metrics

financials.characteristics: Benefits from higher rates (net interest margin), sensitive to credit quality
financials.worst_phase: Recession (credit losses), very low rate environment
financials.indicators: Yield curve slope, loan growth, credit default rates, housing activity

energy.characteristics: Highly correlated to oil price, capital intensive
energy.indicators: Oil inventory levels, rig count, producer break-even costs

healthcare.best_phase: Recession, early bear market — defensive allocation
healthcare.worst_phase: Strong bull markets (underperforms but doesn't lose)
healthcare.indicators: Drug pipeline, FDA approvals, aging demographics

consumer_discretionary.characteristics: Luxury, retail, autos — sensitive to consumer confidence
consumer_discretionary.indicators: Consumer confidence, credit card spending, housing starts

consumer_staples.indicators: Commodity input costs, pricing power, private label competition

utilities.best_phase: Rate cutting cycles, recession (defensive with yield)
utilities.worst_phase: Rate hiking cycles (dividend yield less attractive vs bonds)
utilities.indicators: Interest rates, power demand, renewable transition

industrials.indicators: PMI, freight volumes, factory orders, ISM manufacturing

materials.best_phase: Late expansion, commodity supercycles, infrastructure spending
materials.indicators: Commodity prices, Chinese manufacturing PMI, global trade volumes

real_estate.characteristics: REITs — rate sensitive, benefits from inflation
real_estate.indicators: Interest rates, vacancy rates, rent growth, cap rates

top_down_approach.description: Start from the broadest context and work down to the specific trade

questions: What is the global economic cycle phase?
What is Federal Reserve policy stance?
What is the bond market signaling?
What is the VIX level and trend?
Which sectors are showing relative strength?
Is money flowing into cyclicals or defensives?
What are sector ETFs doing relative to the index?
Within strong sectors, which stocks are leaders?
What is the relative strength vs the sector?
Is the stock building a proper base?
What is the upcoming catalyst (earnings, product launch)?
What is the exact pattern forming?
What are the precise entry, stop, and target?
Is there volume and indicator confirmation?

performance_benchmarking.rule: Never evaluate your performance in absolute terms — always compare to the relevant benchmark AND to risk taken

benchmarks.crypto: Bitcoin or total crypto market cap

performance_benchmarking.outperformance_quality: Making 30% in a year when the market made 30% is zero outperformance. Making 20% when the market made 0% is excellent.
performance_benchmarking.alpha: Return above the benchmark, adjusted for risk taken (beta)
performance_benchmarking.beta: Correlation and sensitivity to the benchmark. Beta 1.5 = 50% more volatile than benchmark.

compound_growth_mathematics.the_rule_of_72: Divide 72 by your annual return to get years to double. 10% return = 7.2 years to double.

power_of_compounding.lesson: Marginal increases in consistent annual return have extraordinary long-term impact

the_cost_of_drawdown.lesson: Protecting capital is MORE important than maximizing gains — the mathematics of loss recovery are brutal
